value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Foreigners can use Visit Japan Web for immigration procedures (immigration clearance, customs declaration) and tax-free purchases during their stay in Japan.

All foreign nationals who need to obtain a short-term visa to Japan and currently reside in Australia, Brazil, Cambodia, Canada, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Taiwan, United Kingdom, or U.S.A. are eligible to apply for a tourist visa online via the JAPAN eVISA system.

Emergency medical assistance services in Japan for foreign visitors include medical referrals, medical monitoring, assistance with reporting theft or loss of travel documents, emergency patient transports, hospital arrangements for evacuated or repatriated patients, and emergency evacuation assistance.
Tourists can bring in goods for personal use purchased abroad whose total market value is less than 200,000 Yen duty-free.
Himeji Castle charges 1,000 yen ($7.01) for local residents and 2,500 yen ($17.52) for visitors from outside the city
Average daily travel cost including accommodation is about $110 per day, with accommodation cost roughly half of that
Most IC cards require a security deposit of 500 yen when purchasing the card.
The Japan Rail Pass is available only to non-Japanese nationals visiting Japan on a tourist visa (Temporary Visitor status).
Chain restaurants like Yoshinoya or Sukiya offer beef bowls and other quick meals starting at around 400 to 600 yen.
Suica cards can be used nationwide in Japan due to the IC card interoperability system, allowing a Suica purchased in Tokyo to be used on subways in Osaka and buses in Fukuoka.
Capsule hotels are available in Matsumoto, Tokyo, and Fukuoka
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
Sea freight shipping from China to Japan typically takes about 7 to 14 days

Pasmo and Suica are rechargeable prepaid rail cards widely used in the Tokyo area and can be purchased at ticket vending machines in stations throughout Japan including major airports such as Tokyo’s Narita and Haneda.
Japan is an island country in East Asia, located in the Pacific Ocean
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Starting March 1, 2026, Himeji Castle more than doubled its admission price for visitors who don’t live in Himeji City, raising the cost from 1,000 yen ($7.01) to 2,500 yen ($17.52)
Iki Retreat by Onko Chishin is located in the heart of Kyoto, set on a river close to major attractions
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
Many popular attractions in Japan become unbearably crowded by midday, so it is recommended to visit attractions early, such as arriving at Senso-ji temple by 9 am to avoid crowds.
Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka, Kanazawa, Yokohama, Oita, Fukuoka
Saying “itadakimasu” before a meal is a way of expressing gratitude in Japanese dining etiquette, showing respect for the culture and preserving Japanese heritage.

Pocket Wi-Fi rental services in Japan can be purchased online with options such as Wi-FiRental.com offering plans from ¥6,600/month with K4/Wimax/SoftBank plans, and Ninja WiFi Infinite Unlimited available for single-day or 7-day rentals at ¥1,980 per day with unlimited data and 4G/LTE or 5G speeds.
Lunch in Japan can cost anywhere from about 400 yen for a simple fast-food meal to around 1,200 yen for a more complete set meal.
Travelers should review coverage maps during trip planning and pre-arrange internet access such as Japan eSIM plans to support multi-city travel and quick navigation through transport systems.
Travelers should review coverage maps during trip planning to ensure reliable mobile internet access in Japan.
In Japan, customers can make purchases via QR code payments using platforms such as PayPay, which allow fast money transfers through smartphones.
Long-term pocket Wi-Fi rentals (30+ days) in Japan can cost as low as $3 per day
Japan uses many international payment methods such as Apple Pay and Google Pay, as well as domestic payment methods like PayPay, which is a popular QR code payment platform.
Budget daily spend: approximately ¥8,000–12,000/day ($50–$75)
Tokyo
Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, and requires the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food.
Credit cards such as Visa, JCB, and Mastercard are widely accepted in Japan, but travelers should be prepared to pay with cash as a backup due to possible payment errors.
Credit cards and debit cards are accepted in most places in Japanese cities, although Japan is primarily a cash society.

Merchants accepting payments in Japan typically receive their funds settled in Japanese Yen (JPY), with payout cycles of 2–5 business days and PSP fees ranging from 2.0% to 3.5% per transaction. PSPs support local payment methods including convenience store payments (konbini), bank transfers, and QR code-based wallets.

Japan is a cash society, but credit cards and debit cards are accepted in most places in Japanese cities. It is recommended to compare travel credit cards before the trip to find one that best suits your spending habits.
Japan is a cash society, but credit cards and debit cards are accepted in most places in Japanese cities.
The Japan Rail Pass can be purchased for durations of 7 days, 14 days, or 21 days.
Each Suica is valid for 180 days from the issuance date (Welcome Suica Mobile).
Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen can be covered in just over two hours
Credit cards are the most convenient method of payment in Japan, with commonly accepted cards including Visa, JCB, and Mastercard.
IC cards can be used to pay for fares on trains, subways, buses, and ferries in Japan.
A taxi ride from Haneda Airport to Tokyo takes around 30-40 minutes.

When eating in Tokyo, to experience authentic Japanese dining, it is recommended to eat like a local by stepping outside usual tourist zones, trying counter dining, following locals, and being adventurous to find hidden gems and neighborhood restaurants.
Mobile Suica apps are targeted at residents of Japan and are difficult or impossible to use for visitors to Japan.
A deposit is required to buy an IC travel card in Japan.

You need to purchase a JR Pass Exchange Order from an authorized sales agent outside Japan prior to your arrival in Japan, then exchange the Order for the JR Pass at Japan Rail Pass exchange offices in Japan within 3 months of issue.
Physical Suica cards must be purchased in Japan and cannot be bought online before arrival; they are commonly available at airports (e.g., Welcome Suica) or train stations upon arrival.
All JR stations display station names in both Japanese and romanized English on their platforms, and some stations use station numbering.
PayPay is a mobile payment service in Japan that allows cashless transactions via smartphones using QR code payments, enabling fast money transfers.
Visa cards are widely accepted in Japan, allowing purchases, payments, and withdrawals at banks and ATMs.
It is recommended to reserve Shinkansen bullet train seats in advance, especially during peak seasons such as summer holidays, cherry blossom season, and New Year.
